title: Quiz 3
description: This quiz covers generics, traits, lifetimes, iterators, and smart pointers (Chapters 13-18).
instructions: Implement a generic report card system using traits and generics.
hints: The ReportCard struct is generic over T, which represents the grade type.
The trait bound `T: Display` ensures the grade can be formatted as a string.
This code should compile - study how generics with trait bounds work.
explanation: This quiz demonstrates generics with trait bounds. `ReportCard<T>` can hold any grade type (f32, &str, etc.) as long as it implements `Display`. The `T: Display` bound on the impl block ensures `format!` can print the grade. The same struct works with numeric grades (3.5) and letter grades ("A+") without code duplication.
